tldr: Georg Zimmermann (Lotto-Intermarché) won at home in Frankfurt, sprinting from inside a 12-rider front group with Tom Pidcock second and Visma's Ben Tulett third. A reduced-bunch dash to the line on the German May Day classic, with the front group holding clear of the chasing peloton.

narrative.headline: Zimmermann at home, Pidcock and Tulett complete the podium
narrative.openingMoves: 211 km from Eschborn into Frankfurt, the German May Day staple. A reduced 12-rider front group survived clear of the peloton.
narrative.raceUnfolds: Standard Eschborn pattern — the climbing into Königstein-area trims the bunch, the front group establishes itself, the peloton's chase doesn't quite close.
narrative.decision: Inside the closing kilometre, the 12-up sprint goes to the rider with the best closing kick and the local crowd. Zimmermann had both.
narrative.finale: Zimmermann won from the back of the group, sprinting along the barriers. Pidcock second, Tulett third — a clean podium spread.

teamStories.narrative: Home win for the German rider in his fifth year as a professional. Lotto-Intermarché's calendar leans on these single-race days; this was the season's biggest return.
Pidcock's spring has had three runner-up finishes (Sanremo by centimetres, Eschborn here, plus another). The pattern of the wrong margin is becoming notable.

riderPerformances.name: Tom Pidcock
riderPerformances.narrative: Second again. After Milan-San Remo by centimetres, Pidcock's spring of close calls continues — Eschborn-Frankfurt second behind Zimmermann.

aftermath.headline: German classic, German winner
aftermath.body: Eschborn-Frankfurt rarely produces a Monument-class headline; what it produces is reliable mid-tier Classics results. Zimmermann is the second German to win this in five years.
